engineIntelligence.h2: Mazda CX-3 Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Mazda CX-3 in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Mazda CX-3 S5 engine - diesel 1.5L replacement UK
productionHistory: Mazda CX-3 1.5 Skyactiv-D 115 (Sport Nav, GT Sport) - 2015-2021
knownFailures: DPF failure - blockage causes black smoke, power loss and frequent regeneration attempts on short journeys.
Injector leak - diesel contaminates engine oil leading to overfilling and potential bearing damage.
Runaway diesel - uncontrolled RPM surge due to oil ingestion; recall affected 2015-2017 models.

cards.altText: Mazda CX-3 PE engine - petrol 2.0L replacement UK
productionHistory: Mazda CX-3 2.0 Skyactiv-G 150 (GT Sport, GT Sport Tech) - 2015-2021
knownFailures: Water pump failure - coolant leaks reported on higher-mileage units; inspect at 80k+ miles.
Timing chain stretch - rare but possible on neglected units; listen for rattles on cold start.
No widely reported catastrophic failure pattern specific to this model - general wear items apply.

cards.altText: Mazda CX-3 S5-DPTR engine - diesel 1.5L 115hp replacement UK
knownFailures: Exhaust control flap failure - prevents DPF regeneration leading to blockage and power loss.
Fuel filter clogging - causes power loss; replace before condemning the engine.
Oil dilution from injector leak - rising oil level indicates diesel contamination; act immediately.

engineGuide.h2: Mazda CX-3 Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Mazda CX-3 across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 1.5L Diesel
entries.history: Entry-level 105bhp diesel for CX-3; common in SE-L trims, DPF issues dominate replacement demand.
compatibleVariants: SE-L, SE-L Nav, Sport, Sport Nav (2WD), Sport Nav (AWD 2015-2019)
commonFailures: DPF blockage on short journeys causing smoke and power loss
Injector leak leading to oil dilution and rising dipstick level
Exhaust control flap failure preventing regeneration
entries.cta: Get quotes for S5-DPTS Mazda CX-3 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.5L Diesel
entries.history: Higher-output 115bhp variant for Sport Nav and GT Sport; same failure patterns as S5-DPTS.
compatibleVariants: Sport Nav, Sport Nav+, GT Sport, GT Sport Tech (2WD and AWD where available)
commonFailures: High-pressure fuel pump wear causing contamination
Oil overfill from injector failure destroying bearings
entries.cta: Get quotes for S5-DPTR Mazda CX-3 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.5L Diesel Regional Variants
entries.history: Export-market S5 codes occasionally seen on UK imports; identical failure profile to S5-DPTS.
compatibleVariants: Imported CX-3 models with European or Australian specification
commonFailures: DPF failure and injector issues identical to UK-spec S5 engines
Verify exact code via VIN before ordering replacement
entries.cta: Get quotes for S5Y2/S5Y5 Mazda CX-3 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: Reliable 120bhp naturally aspirated petrol; backbone of CX-3 petrol range with fewer catastrophic failures.
compatibleVariants: SE-L, SE-L Nav, Sport, Sport Nav, Sport Nav+ (2WD and AWD)
commonFailures: Water pump seal wear causing coolant leaks on higher-mileage units
Timing chain tensioner wear (rare, usually after 100k miles)
No widespread catastrophic failure pattern specific to CX-3 application
entries.cta: Get quotes for PE-VPR Mazda CX-3 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: Higher-output 150bhp variant for GT Sport trims; same robust architecture as PE-VPR.
compatibleVariants: GT Sport, GT Sport Tech (2WD and AWD)
commonFailures: Water pump and thermostat housing leaks reported on 80k+ mile examples
Generally reliable; replacement demand driven by accident damage rather than failure

commonProblems.h2: Mazda CX-3 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Mazda CX-3's 1.5 Skyactiv-D diesel dominates UK replacement enquiries due to DPF, injector and runaway diesel issues, while the 2.0 Skyactiv-G petrol remains notably reliable. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the cost comparisons below to decide between repair and replacement.

problems.h4: DPF Failure - Why Mazda CX-3 1.5 Diesel Engines Lose Power
problems.affectedModels: Mazda CX-3 SE-L, Sport Nav, GT Sport 1.5 Skyactiv-D (2015-2021, S5-DPTS & S5-DPTR)
problems.rootCause: Diesel particulate filter cannot regenerate on frequent short trips; soot accumulates until blockage triggers limp mode, black smoke and power loss.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Chemical clean or diagnostic forced regeneration attempt
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix if blockage is mild; not viable if filter is physically damaged

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove old DPF, fit new or reconditioned unit with sensors
repairOptions.longevity: Restores function but does not address root cause of frequent short journeys

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded components addressing DPF/injector issues
repairOptions.longevity: Best value when DPF failure has caused secondary damage; includes 12-month warranty

problems.recommendation: If your CX-3 shows early DPF warning lights and you primarily drive short distances, a forced regeneration may buy time. However, if black smoke, power loss or oil overfill are present, the DPF failure has likely caused secondary damage - a rebuilt S5 engine with updated components is often more cost-effective than repeated DPF repairs on a car worth £8,000-£15,000.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt S5 diesel engine prices for your Mazda CX-3

problems.h4: Runaway Diesel Recall - Catastrophic Failure on 2015-2017 CX-3
problems.affectedModels: Mazda CX-3 1.5 Skyactiv-D all trims (Jan 2015-Nov 2017, S5-DPTS & S5-DPTR)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: Any mileage; triggered by oil ingestion rather than wear
problems.rootCause: Faulty software allows unburnt diesel to enter crankcase; engine ingests its own oil and revs uncontrollably until mechanical destruction occurs.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Mazda dealer applies updated ECU software to prevent runaway condition
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if engine has NOT yet suffered runaway; check recall status immediately

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with updated software and reinforced internals
repairOptions.longevity: Only option once runaway has occurred; engine is typically destroyed beyond repair

problems.recommendation: If your CX-3 is within the 2015-2017 recall window and has not yet suffered runaway, contact Mazda immediately for the free software update. If the engine has already run away, repair is not possible - a rebuilt S5 engine with the updated software pre-installed is the only viable solution.
problems.cta: Get quotes for recalled 2015-2017 Mazda CX-3 diesel engine replacement

problems.h4: Injector Failure & Oil Dilution - Silent Engine Killer
problems.affectedModels: Mazda CX-3 1.5 Skyactiv-D all trims (2015-2021, S5-DPTS & S5-DPTR)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 70,000-120,000 miles; can occur earlier with poor fuel quality
problems.rootCause: Worn injector seals allow diesel to leak into crankcase; oil level rises, viscosity drops, and bearing lubrication fails.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove injectors, replace seals and recalibrate
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if caught before oil dilution causes bearing damage; requires specialist diagnostics

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit new injectors, flush contaminated oil, replace filter
repairOptions.longevity: May save engine if dilution is mild and addressed immediately; risk of residual damage remains

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded injectors and oil system components
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended once oil level has risen above max mark or knocking is heard; prevents repeat failure

problems.recommendation: Check your CX-3's oil level monthly. If the dipstick shows oil above the max mark or smells strongly of diesel, stop driving immediately. Injector seal replacement is only cost-effective if caught before bearing damage occurs; once knocking is audible, a rebuilt engine is the only reliable solution.
problems.cta: Find rebuilt Mazda CX-3 S5 engines with upgraded injectors

problems.h4: Exhaust Control Flap Failure - Hidden DPF Blocker
problems.affectedModels: Mazda CX-3 1.5 Skyactiv-D Sport Nav, GT Sport (2015-2021, S5-DPTR)
problems.rootCause: Plastic actuator arm on exhaust flap breaks or motor fails; flap sticks closed preventing DPF regeneration.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace broken actuator arm or motor; reset adaptations
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if DPF is not yet blocked; quick fix on accessible S5 engines

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove faulty flap mechanism, clean DPF, remap ECU to ignore flap
repairOptions.longevity: Cost-effective but may trigger warning lights; ensure MOT compliance

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine when flap failure has caused severe DPF blockage
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if DPF is physically damaged or engine has suffered secondary issues

problems.recommendation: If your CX-3 shows persistent DPF warnings but no smoke, suspect the exhaust flap first - it's a cheaper fix than DPF replacement. However, if power loss and black smoke are present, the DPF is likely blocked and a rebuilt engine may offer better long-term value than repeated component repairs.
problems.cta: Compare Mazda CX-3 S5 engine prices with exhaust system upgrades

problems.h4: 2.0 Petrol Reliability - When Replacement Is Rarely Needed
problems.affectedModels: Mazda CX-3 2.0 Skyactiv-G all trims (2015-2021, PE-VPR & PE-VPS)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 100,000+ miles for major issues; generally robust
problems.rootCause: No widespread design flaw; replacement demand usually stems from accident damage, neglect, or rare water pump failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace leaking water pump or thermostat housing; refill coolant
repairOptions.longevity: Cost-effective preventative maintenance on 80k+ mile petrol CX-3s

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine for accident-damaged or neglected units
repairOptions.longevity: Rarely needed for failure; best option when engine is physically damaged beyond repair

problems.recommendation: The 2.0 Skyactiv-G is one of Mazda's most reliable modern engines. Unless your CX-3 has suffered accident damage or severe neglect, repair is almost always more cost-effective than replacement. For peace of mind on high-mileage examples, a rebuilt PE engine with upgraded water pump offers excellent longevity.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt PE petrol engine prices for your Mazda CX-3

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Mazda CX-3
finalCta.paragraph: The Mazda CX-3's compact size and strong residual values (£8,000-£15,000) make engine replacement a smart alternative to scrappage. EngineMarket connects you with UK specialists who supply rebuilt S5 diesel and PE petrol engines with upgraded components addressing known failure points. Every unit includes a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery - compare prices today and keep your CX-3 on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Mazda CX-3 Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Mazda CX-3 and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineTypes.intro: Every Mazda CX-3 engine type is available through our UK network with typical price ranges based on variant, engine code and budget.

types.title: Mazda CX-3 Reconditioned Engines (Recon)
types.description: Professionally cleaned, inspected and repaired Mazda CX-3 engines with worn parts replaced.
types.frontDescription: Professionally cleaned, inspected and repaired Mazda CX-3 engines with worn parts replaced.
types.frontDisclaimer: Suitability depends on core engine condition and failure type.
types.backDescription: Reconditioned Mazda CX-3 engines undergo strip, clean and inspection with gaskets, seals and worn components replaced. Ideal for S5 diesel units with DPF/injector issues where the block is sound.
backBullets: Includes new gaskets, seals and oil pump
Faster turnaround than full rebuild for suitable cores
types.cta: Compare reconditioned Mazda CX-3 engine prices

types.title: Mazda CX-3 Rebuilt Engines
types.description: Comprehensive rebuild of Mazda CX-3 engines with upgraded components addressing known failures.
types.frontDescription: Comprehensive rebuild of Mazda CX-3 engines with upgraded components addressing known failures.
types.frontDisclaimer: Best value for catastrophic failures or high-mileage units.
types.backDescription: Rebuilt Mazda CX-3 engines feature upgraded pistons, bearings and injectors to address S5 diesel weaknesses. Recommended for runaway diesel, severe oil dilution or accident-damaged PE petrol units.
backBullets: Upgraded components target known failure points
Suitable for both S5 diesel and PE petrol CX-3 engines

types.frontDisclaimer: Mileage and history vary; warranty terms differ by supplier.
types.backDescription: Used Mazda CX-3 engines offer budget replacement for S5 diesel or PE petrol units where the donor vehicle had low miles. Verify compression, service history and warranty before purchase.
backBullets: Significant cost saving vs rebuilt for suitable applications
Always request compression test and service records
3-6 month warranty typical; confirm terms before ordering

engineSizes.h2: Mazda CX-3 Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Mazda CX-3 in the UK is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the correct replacement.

items.title: Mazda CX-3 1.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.5L Skyactiv-D (S5 family) powered all UK Mazda CX-3 diesel variants from launch in 2015 through to 2021. Available in 105bhp and 115bhp outputs, this turbocharged unit delivered strong economy but is associated with DPF, injector and runaway diesel issues that dominate replacement enquiries.
commonFailurePoints: exhaust control flap failure preventing regeneration
items.cta: Get quotes for Mazda CX-3 1.5L diesel engines

items.title: Mazda CX-3 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0L Skyactiv-G (PE family) was the sole petrol option for the UK Mazda CX-3 throughout its 2015-2021 production. Offered in 120bhp and 150bhp naturally aspirated forms, this robust engine has far fewer catastrophic failure reports than the diesel, with replacement demand typically driven by accident damage rather than design flaws.
commonFailurePoints: Water pump seal wear on high-mileage units
rare timing chain tensioner wear after 100k miles
items.cta: Compare Mazda CX-3 2.0L petrol engine prices

engineSizes.closing: Not sure which engine size your Mazda CX-3 needs? Enter your registration number above - we'll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Mazda CX-3 specialists.

engineYears.h2: Mazda CX-3 Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers all major engine changes across the Mazda CX-3's UK production run (2015-2021), organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: Launch Year: S5 Diesel & PE Petrol Debut
years.description: The CX-3 launched in the UK with two engine families: the 1.5L Skyactiv-D diesel (S5) and 2.0L Skyactiv-G petrol (PE), both carrying known failure risks that would shape replacement demand.
keyChanges: Mazda CX-3 introduced to UK market on DK platform
Both 1.5L diesel (105/115bhp) and 2.0L petrol (120/150bhp) available from launch
AWD offered on select diesel and petrol variants
knownFor: Strong initial sales driven by SUV styling and Skyactiv efficiency
Early reports of DPF issues on diesel models used for short journeys
Petrol engines praised for refinement and reliability
Runaway diesel recall announced for Jan-Nov 2017 production (affects 2015 builds)
years.cta: Get quotes for 2015 Mazda CX-3 replacement engines
years.ticker: Mazda CX-3 1.5 diesel engine replacement • Mazda CX-3 DPF failure • Mazda CX-3 runaway diesel recall

years.preview: Recall Period: Runaway Diesel Software Update
years.description: These model years saw the runaway diesel recall affect early CX-3s, while DPF and injector issues on the S5 diesel began driving significant replacement enquiries.
keyChanges: Mazda issued recall for 2015-2017 CX-3 diesel models to update ECU software
Sport Nav+ trim introduced with enhanced equipment
No mechanical changes to engine families; failure patterns became clearer
knownFor: Runaway diesel condition dominated owner forum discussions
DPF failure reports increased as first owners reached 60k miles
Petrol CX-3s maintained strong reliability reputation
AWD variants popular in rural areas but added complexity to engine access
years.cta: Compare Mazda CX-3 2016-2017 engine replacement prices
years.ticker: Mazda CX-3 2016 diesel engine recall • Mazda CX-3 injector failure oil leak • Mazda CX-3 2.0 petrol engine cost
enquiries: Mazda CX-3 injector failure oil leak

years.preview: Facelift Era: Minor Updates, Same Engines
years.description: The 2018 facelift brought styling and equipment updates but no engine changes; S5 diesel failure patterns remained consistent while PE petrol reliability continued to impress.
keyChanges: Exterior refresh with revised grille, lights and bumpers
Enhanced infotainment and safety features across range
No changes to S5 diesel or PE petrol engine specifications
mainEngines: PE-VPS - 2.0L petrol 150hp (GT Sport, GT Sport Tech)
popularModels: Mazda CX-3 GT Sport Tech 2.0 Petrol
knownFor: Facelift models retained same engine failure patterns as pre-facelift
DPF issues still prevalent on diesel models used for urban commuting
Petrol CX-3s increasingly sought as reliable used buys
Residual values remained strong (£10k-£15k) supporting replacement investment
years.cta: Get quotes for 2018-2019 Mazda CX-3 replacement engines

years.preview: Final Production: Diesel Phased Out in Some Markets
years.description: As CX-3 production wound down, diesel variants became less common in some markets while petrol models remained popular; replacement demand focused on high-mileage S5 units.
keyChanges: Diesel variants discontinued in some European markets; UK retained both fuel types
Final GT Sport Tech editions launched with enhanced equipment
No new engine codes introduced; focus on clearing existing stock
mainEngines: S5-DPTR - 1.5L diesel 115hp (Sport Nav+, GT Sport - limited availability)
PE-VPS - 2.0L petrol 150hp (GT Sport, GT Sport Tech)
popularModels: Mazda CX-3 GT Sport Tech 2.0 Petrol
Mazda CX-3 GT Sport 1.5 Diesel (final batches)
knownFor: Late-model petrol CX-3s highly desirable for reliability and equipment
Remaining diesel units still subject to DPF/injector failure patterns
Strong residual values (£8k-£15k) made engine replacement economically viable
Final-year models often low-mileage; replacement demand shifted to earlier high-mileage examples

engineYears.closing: Not sure which year your Mazda CX-3 was built? Enter your registration number and we'll identify the exact engine, model year, and compatible replacement options instantly - all backed by a minimum 12‑month warranty from UK Mazda CX-3 specialists.

faq.h2: Mazda CX-3 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Mazda CX-3 engine?
items.answer: Rebuilt Mazda CX-3 engines typically cost £1,800-£3,500 for the 1.5L diesel (S5) and £2,200-£4,200 for the 2.0L petrol (PE), supply only. Supply & fit adds £600-£1,200 depending on location and garage rates.
keyPoints: Diesel S5 engines cheaper due to higher failure volume and parts availability
Petrol PE engines cost more but fail less often
Prices vary by engine code, variant and supplier warranty terms
Always compare multiple quotes before committing
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Mazda CX-3 engine prices from UK specialists

items.question: Which Mazda CX-3 engine is most reliable?
items.answer: The 2.0L Skyactiv-G petrol (PE family) is significantly more reliable than the 1.5L Skyactiv-D diesel (S5 family), with far fewer catastrophic failure reports in UK owner forums and specialist networks.
keyPoints: PE petrol engines rarely suffer runaway or DPF-related destruction
S5 diesel engines have documented DPF, injector and exhaust flap issues
Petrol CX-3s often reach 100k+ miles without major engine work
Diesel replacement demand is driven by specific failure patterns, not age
items.cta: Find reliable Mazda CX-3 petrol engine replacement options

items.question: What causes Mazda CX-3 diesel engines to fail?
items.answer: DPF blockage from short journeys, injector leaks causing oil dilution, and the 2015-2017 runaway diesel recall are the three most common causes of catastrophic failure on Mazda CX-3 1.5L Skyactiv-D engines.
keyPoints: DPF cannot regenerate on frequent short trips, leading to blockage
Faulty injectors leak diesel into crankcase, destroying bearings
Runaway condition occurs when engine ingests its own oil
All three issues can destroy the engine if not addressed immediately
items.cta: Get quotes for Mazda CX-3 S5 diesel engine replacement with upgraded components

items.question: Is it worth replacing a Mazda CX-3 engine?
items.answer: Yes, if your CX-3 is in good condition elsewhere and valued at £8,000-£15,000, a rebuilt engine (£1,800-£4,200) is often more cost-effective than part-exchanging or scrapping the vehicle.
keyPoints: CX-3 residual values support investment in engine replacement
Rebuilt engines include 12-month warranty for peace of mind
Supply & fit options available nationwide through specialist network
Compare quotes to ensure you get best value for your variant
items.cta: Calculate if Mazda CX-3 engine replacement makes financial sense

items.question: Can I fit a Mazda CX-3 engine myself?
items.answer: Professional fitting is strongly recommended for Mazda CX-3 engines due to Skyactiv-specific procedures, DPF/injector coding requirements and the risk of costly errors on a complex modern powertrain.
keyPoints: Skyactiv engines require specialist tools and diagnostic equipment
DPF and injector systems need ECU coding after replacement
Incorrect installation can void warranty and cause secondary damage
Supply & fit quotes include labour, coding and road test
items.cta: Find Mazda CX-3 engine supply & fit specialists near you

items.question: How do I find my Mazda CX-3 engine code?
items.answer: Your Mazda CX-3 engine code (e.g., S5-DPTS, PE-VPR) is printed on the engine block near the oil filter housing, listed on your V5C logbook under "engine number", or can be identified instantly by entering your registration above.
keyPoints: Engine code is critical for ordering compatible replacement units
S5 codes denote 1.5L diesel; PE codes denote 2.0L petrol
DVLA lookup via registration is fastest and most reliable method
Our specialists can verify code compatibility before dispatch
items.cta: Use our Mazda CX-3 engine code lookup tool

items.question: What warranty comes with Mazda CX-3 replacement engines?
items.answer: Every rebuilt, reconditioned or remanufactured Mazda CX-3 engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK specialists, with some offering 24-month coverage on remanufactured units.
keyPoints: Warranty covers parts and labour for manufacturing defects
Unlimited mileage terms apply across the UK network
Always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier before ordering
Warranty documentation provided with every engine dispatch
items.cta: Compare Mazda CX-3 engine warranty terms from UK suppliers

items.question: How long does Mazda CX-3 engine replacement take?
items.answer: Delivery of a rebuilt Mazda CX-3 engine typically takes 2-5 working days; professional fitting at a specialist garage usually requires 6-8 hours for petrol units and 8-10 hours for diesel due to DPF/injector coding.
keyPoints: Supply-only engines dispatched within 48 hours of order confirmation
Fitting time varies by variant, drivetrain and garage workload
Diesel units require additional time for emissions system coding
Many specialists offer courtesy cars or collection/delivery services
items.cta: Get a timeline quote for your Mazda CX-3 engine replacement

items.question: Are Mazda CX-3 petrol and diesel engines interchangeable?
items.answer: No, Mazda CX-3 1.5L diesel (S5) and 2.0L petrol (PE) engines are completely different designs and cannot be swapped without extensive modification to wiring, fuel system, exhaust and ECU - not cost-effective.
keyPoints: S5 diesel and PE petrol have different mounting points and electronics
Conversion would require custom wiring loom and ECU remapping
Exhaust, fuel tank and emissions systems are fuel-type specific
Stick with the original fuel type for your CX-3 variant
items.cta: Find compatible Mazda CX-3 engine replacements for your fuel type

items.question: What if my Mazda CX-3 was affected by the runaway diesel recall?
items.answer: If your 2015-2017 Mazda CX-3 diesel hasn't had the recall software update, contact Mazda immediately for the free fix; if the engine has already run away, a rebuilt S5 engine with updated software is the only viable replacement option.
keyPoints: Recall applies to CX-3 diesel models built Jan 2015-Nov 2017
Free software update prevents runaway if engine is still intact
Once runaway occurs, engine is typically destroyed beyond repair
Rebuilt S5 engines from our network include updated ECU software pre-installed
items.cta: Check recall status and get quotes for Mazda CX-3 runaway diesel replacement
